review_text: An amazing stay. Stayed at the family unit with 3 bedrooms. The cabin was comparable to a 5 star hotel room. From the spacious living room, to the very comfy bedrooms, stone benchtops kitchen, pots and pans, appliances, everything were well thought out and made it a very classy quality stay. The water pressure was amazing and plenty of hot water for 2 families with kids. We were very lucky to get the end cabin facing the hills and view was amazing. So much to do with peddle gokarts, jumping cushions, games room and more. The service was absolutely fantastic.

Just fabulous considering the are recovering from severe floods in January. We have a new cabin & it’s wonderful surrounded by so many kangaroos. Fabulous place for families with playground, bouncy something… sorry but not sure what it is but looks like fun. Running stream going out to the ocean, walks: honestly this is just special.

What a fantastic getaway! The vibe is incredible and unforgettable. The campfires and daily food trucks made our stay so enjoyable and easy. From lake to beach! Can’t get any better than that. Will definitely be back again
Lovely tranquil park and clean amenities. Our fruits and vegetables were kept in boxes and latched. The cockatoos still managed to open the lids and ate our fruits. Campers beware.
Had a wonderful Easter Break down here with family. Loved the new family cabins
Absolutrly the best big 4 in australia. Great hospitality, great services. 10/10 would recommend
Had a very good experience with Big 4 Wye River. Staff were exceptional. Friendly, and very accomodating. The park and facilities very clean. Very happy

We stayed here for 3 days and had a really wonderful experience. From the moment we arrived, the reception staff were super friendly and welcoming, which gave us a great first impression. Check-in was smooth, and they kindly explained everything we needed to know.
The park is very well maintained and clean. All the facilities, including the bathrooms, showers, and common areas, were spotless and clearly well looked after. Even though it’s surrounded by nature, everything felt tidy and comfortable. It’s a great place for families, couples, or friends, and we would definitely love to come back again. Highly recommended!
Cabins deliver a true 'glamping' experience with clean modern interiors and a full kitchen. The lovely staff ensured we had a positive experience. I would come back here again with friends

Such an upgrade from our last visit! Big range of accommodation available with the newer cabins having some amazing features such as bathroom floor heating! The park itself if massive with a games room, gym, coffee truck, playground, and more. The staff were all also very lovely and welcoming. Really enjoyed a relaxing weekend here and I’m planning on coming back to try their safari tents as well. :)

A beautiful park that you'll want to come back to. Hard to tell it hosted a natural disaster a fortnight ago, we sat by the river, birdlife and kangaroos everywhere to be seen. Individual firepits at most sites. An over-zealous kookaburra stole my friend's sausage right out of her hand! They come THAT close! Cool bar located in a converted 1960s caravan serves slushies, cans, coffee and hot chocolate. A cool musical trio played covers on bass, guitar and fiddle. Didn't have need of the enormous camp kitchen or playground. Stayed in a very comfortable, well appointed (coffee, tea, stove, crockery, utensils, etc.), brand-new cabin decorated in white, charcoal and walnut, with forest-floor views out large picture-windows.  Luxury mattresses and white linen. What a luxury weekend. Thanks.

We have stayed at Big 4 Wye River for eight years and were shocked by the lack of any warning or communication during the 2026 floods. Guests were left uninformed in a dangerous situation.

This was irresponsible and unacceptable. After years of loyalty, your failure to act shows a clear disregard for guest safety and has completely destroyed our trust.

We had a van site next to the river. Delightful. Facilities top notch and very clean. Showers have plenty of hot water and pressure. This is our second visit👍. A Lovley walk around the perimeter of the site.
stunning location where river & country meets ocean coast. the ultimate place where everything you need is here.
Excellent location. All the staff are very friendly. Especially the receptionist sharn was very kind and helpful. Thank you very much
The park itself was nice, but after 10 months on the road it was the staff that stood out for us, very accommodating. Thanks guys, I’m sure we’ll be back.

Wir sind uns nach 2 Monaten Australien Camperrundreise einig: Das war der schönste unserer Plätze!
Viele Stellplätze, Picknicktische, moderne und super gepflegte (und sogar beheizte(!)) Einrichtungen, eine große, geschlossene Küche mit allem, was das Herz begehrt und die Umgebung ist ein Traum! Direkt am Fluss gelegen bietet der Platz Spaziermöglichkeiten und außer Kakadus, Papageien und Kookaburras besuchen auch viele Kängurus den Ort (Es soll auch ab und an Koalas gegeben haben)
Schöne Natur und ein sehr angenehmer Aufenthalt machen.
Einziger nicht so schöner Punkt waren die beiden angebundenen Ziegen… Ich kenne nicht den Grund, deswegen erwähn ich es nur am Rande…

A Fabulous location. Small "town" with a general store, a cafe, TV reception and a hotel right outside the caravan park - what else do you need? The beach is gorgeous, the Big 4 caravan park has everything,  especially for families. It's shady, had a creek running through with ducks, playgrounds, lovely new amenities and dump point. The only negative was the price - very expensive even outside school holidays but......... we stayed anyway.

We stayed here in a campervan with an ensuite. It is such a beautiful place to stay with excellent facilities. We were visited by many native birds (keep and eye on your food at all times!) and saw kangaroos on site also.
Der Beste Campingplatz ! Alles super sauber und so viel Platz und so viele kangaroos und Papagei 🦜 und kangaroos 🦘.
Amazing camp site, we had one with an ensuite which was clean and tidy. Laundry was the best we’ve used on a camp site. Having a concrete slab is also a big positive. Will definitely be back.

Rain Didn’t Stop the Fun!

We took our caravan to BIG4 Wye River and had the best time! The weather was rainy at first (lots of mud!) but the kids still had a blast. The park is beautiful, surrounded by trees with a river running through it. Our ensuite site (32) was spotless, modern, and spacious with a big shower, plenty of hooks, and a heat lamp.

The playground was a hit, the jumping pillow was full of happy kids making friends, and movie night was great too! The go-karts were great fun and good value at $5 for half hour. We saw cockatoos, parrots, and even a few koalas in the trees. The coffee van and café out the front made great coffee and food.
You can hire a fire pit & wood too for an added cost $50 during peak times.
The beach across the road was too rough this weekend to swim, but the kids loved the river entrance and rock pools.

We took a trip to the Otway TreeTop Adventures which was beautiful

We’ll definitely be back!

This is our second visit, and once again we have been stunned by the natural beauty of the area! A small pocket along the Great Ocean Road with native animals everywhere you turn, and for the keen eye, you can spot whales out in Bass Straight.
The cabins are modern and spacious, with a well appointed kitchen and ensuite bathroom.
On the doorstep between Lorne and Apollo Bay there are multiple places to visit within 20 minutes drive.
We took a trip to the Maits Rest Rainforest walk near Cape Otway and found it breath taking and an easy hike with kids.
I highly recommend for a family getaway!

We had an amazing weekend away with our family in The House on The Hill. It was simply beautiful. The park’s facilities were fabulous and the staff were so friendly and helpful. We will certainly be back!

Absolutely stunning spot for a camping getaway. I went with a group of friends during winter and we camped right next to the river that runs through the middle of the park. It was incredibly peaceful and beautiful. The staff were super friendly and welcoming. The beach just across the road was perfect for a morning walk, especially to catch the sunrise. A perfect place to relax, unwind and enjoy nature.
Highly recommend!
Such a great park! Got a free upgrade to the ensuite site before we even arrived! Such friendly staff aswell!

Absolutely brilliant place to stay. We had a girls weekend away for the GOR running festival and found the park to be a perfect location. Heaps of great facilities, friendly staff and a simple but comfortable room. Walking distance to great meals at the pub, a beach across the road, and driving distance to Lorne. Highly recommend for families or just friends.
One of the best big 4 in Victoria

Overall a good place to stay with ample room for everyone to enjoy. The only reason for a lost star is that I found it odd the toilet amenities aren’t near the powered sites but rather where the cabins were (we were situated on the far end of the park). I also had food poisoning from the coffee place which really sucked and was disappointing. Regardless, still a great place to stay and look into.

We have just spent 5 nights there in our van
Whilst the park itself was lovely and we had a good spot we were disappointed.
Yes we were there in school holidays and appreciate it is a kids park, however our concern is the lack of parent supervision with smaller children that should not be allowed to ride on the roads the vehicles travel on. We had a young girl ride right in front of us.. very close and we would never have lived with ourselves if something had happened
We were also disappointed with the amenities
They were good amenities but never seemed clean as there was so many people using them
Floors always dirty, toilets not clean and running out of toilet paper as the amenities weren’t serviced enough
I don’t think we will ever go back and unfortunately would not recommend it to anyone

We love Wye river big4!
The campsite, playground and general areas are fab! It is a pity to charge for 30mins use of buggies, when the kids do love them so much. The older cabins could use with some TLC - they are very tired for the $250++ p/n rate.. especially for a single mama travelling.
Highlights, coffee cart, crepe van, wildlife, buggies, beach, general store!
Top locatie dicht bij het strand. Prachtige ensuite. Alleen erg veel last van de rook rondom ons

My wife and I stayed here for 2 nights because we applied for a job they advertised on seek both Belinda and I were greeted with the lovely ladys at the reception area thay set us a site that wasn't going to work out for pegging our camper thay let us pick the site witch worked for the camper with no fuss we were very impressed with how easy and smoothly it went we had a great spot right beside the river,  the gentleman staff member made us feel very comfortable we had a lovely fire and a couple of late nights around it thank you all for making the visit worthwhile I hope I said enough to the girls and hopefully get looked at for the couples position Thanks again Aaron and belinda,  (the pub grub was also good value and a great view )😁

Great location and super clean facilities. Staff were all wonderful.

Gym was an excellent and well worth the additional $10/day. I’d note it’s only open during reception hours though from memory 0830-1830 weekdays and 0730-1730 Saturday/Sunday which is a bit of a pain for someone who tries to get stuff done before the family wake.

There was a few things like the fire pit, doonas for the bunk beds and karts which were an extra $10 or so.  Notice after paying for our karts, once the bank of them was left unlocked other kids just grabbed them willy nilly without paying.

We stayed in a two bedroom cabin. For those who like cooking while travelling I always find there’s not enough info on what’s in room…the one we stayed in had a dual gas burner cooktop - Small detail but frustrating detail to note, the sauté pan in the cupboards did not sit centred over the large burner at the back as it was too close to the back wall. Tiny but was a pain! 🥲
Put some photos of the cooking kit supplied also as FYI to know what to bring yourself.

Really enjoyed the stay, thanks again!
Great park for the whole family, across the road from the beach. We stayed in the Safari family tent and Loved it.

We stayed on a powered (paved) site  backing onto the creek, mid week (last week) opposite the 'front' amenities block. The current drought is not helping the look of the place but its not too bad. Aside from the amazingly high price these people charge my only main criticism is with the amenities block. It must have been something once and is still pretty reasonable. But in the mens area, there are cracked floor tiles that may eventually cut someones foot one day. And why no toilet brushes? Some people won't use them even after they leave a disgusting mess but there are some of us that like to leave it as we found it (assuming it was clean to start). A small thing. Finally, when was the last time the staff swept outside the entrance doors to the mens and womens toilets? A small thing but so easily done. 3.5 stars is more accurate.

Quite polluted and not well managed for environmental protection or natural enjoyment. The river was thick with algae and smelled terrible, plastic all throughout including caution tape from bridge works the establishment itself was undertaking. No pride in the beautiful area they’re drawing income from.

Would like to see: considerate effort to restore water quality; protect park from plastic rubbish by having more bins, and demarcated bins for different rubbish types accessible in all kitchen areas (currently glass and composting goes in the same bin); warnings about water quality and access to safe water.

Big4 makes a lot of money, and they need to do better. I had high hopes but don’t think we’ll be back even if my child did love the bouncy tramp.

We went last weekend and the kids had so much fun. The amenities were very well kept and clean. Lots of wildlife and very close to the beach. The backyard bar was a bonus for the adults. Would definitely stay here again!

Do not book a grass site as it is literally a dirt site that you will pay $120 )per night. There is no grey water facility in fact I was told they were bring me a bucket that I can then take to the dump point and empty. Other than that the facilities are amazing if you have a powered concrete site or a cabin. PS current map has been updated before a actally facilities installed and will charge 1.5% on eftpos regardless if savings or credit because this is what the bank charges.

Fantastic park with great amenities. Lots of wildlife (ducks, cockatoos, kangaroos and a couple of koalas) and heaps for kids and teenagers.  We had an ensuite site and it was perfect: modern and spotlessly clean ensuite and huge site with concrete and well grassed areas. Easy walk to an excellent Cafe and pub with stunning views.

We took our campervan, and it was the perfect spot for our family with 2 kids. The amenities were fantastic—very clean, well-maintained, and organised. The activities during the school holidays were great, and the whole vibe was really enjoyable. The beach is right in front, and there’s a nice cafe, a pub, and a little store where we found everything we needed. An amazing spot!

Been coming here for 5 years and the whole family love it. It's the only place we've ever repeatedly come to.
The place constantly has new improvement and additions like the gym this year, though most people were surprised it's not free.
Kangaroos can come right up to cabins as does all the other wildlife, including koalas.
For all these reasons, it's very popular and they have a wait list to get in.

A rear place that gets busy at peak times. We have been a couple of times with grand kids and alone. Lots of bird life and walks. Perfect water sports for surfers and beach lovers. A nice pub with good meals overlooking the bay. A lovely spot with a gravel race small river running through the middle. Try to book one of the sites next to the river
Great facilities and helpful staff. Nice and secluded location.
The new gym addition is 11/10!!!!

If you’re a younger group of people and want to be treated rudely and made to feel uncomfortable by the unwelcoming manager Andy, then this is the place for you.

Our stay started with our group of six 18 year olds being stereotyped by the staff, then being asked to pay an extra bond on top of what we had already paid. or the other option being sending for an adult over 25 to join us immediately.

Next, due to the unwelcoming arrival our group asked for the terms and conditions in reference to refunds. after being told a refund was possible if we left that afternoon we started seeking other housing. Later the manager went back on his word and the refund was now not an option. alongside this it was clearly explained to us that if our group received any noise complaints we could be kicked out at any moment without our money back.

This site is riddled with legal faults, conflicting staff and a general dead atmosphere.

Paying over 900 dollars for a four night stay you’d think the place would be clean and have good amenities.

on the contrary! dirty sheets, unwashed cooking utensils, spiders all over the place and broken furniture.

overall. don't book a stay here. it may seem easy but it’s not!!

Terrible stay. Rude Manager.
Big up to this Amazing campground !! Very friendly and welcomed staff. A lot of facilities for having fun ! We went there for their staff party and had a real pleasure ! Thanks guys
Awesome park

This palace was once a Mecca.. but now  lies in a shambles with whatever management has taken over. Bins spill in the open, grass is above ankle high and general cleanliness and maintenance is overlooked for cost cutting by a careless team of people.. this Big 4 does not resemble anything of its previous grandeur. Stick it in the bin of the run of the mill…

Amazing facilities ensuite site was 5 star clean and roomy. Huge concrete site fantastic lawned sites. Creek through the site. Friendly kangaroos. We were driving from Perth to Tasmania and only stopped overnight. But we said we wished this was closer to us as this is somewhere it would be amazing the holiday with kids.
Beautiful holiday park and great for families
Amazing views and excellent amenities

Looks beautiful but for $92.00 per night for an unpowered grass site that "maybe" next to the river is highway robbery. The quoted $52 per site rises by $20 per child per night as soon as you go to the booking page. This is classic false marketing as most groups staying on these sites are self-contained caravanning families who don't use the amenities. Like us. Very disappointing team!
Typical of Big4 parks around the country overcharging Aussie families trying to take an affordable holiday. There is no world where that price is acceptable no matter how "pretty" the park is.

Skip this place and go to the Kennet River Holiday Park just 10mins down the GOR, beautiful little park which is run by a very organised and friendly team. Koalas in the trees. Beach is better too!

Note: I have run my own business, so I fully understand how operations costs are always rising, but to charge nearly 100 bucks per non-powered site per night, this park is really ripping people off.
Great place to set up the van

Lovely staff who greeted us so kindly.
The rain was coming down but that's 'Mother Nature' in control.
It was not holiday season so we could choose our site and we were only having a short stop over on our journey.

Lovely powered site by the river.
The park is great and all amenities so clean and tidy.

We would HIGHLY RECOMMEND
BIG4 WYE RIVER to anyone.
Excellent place to stay for families.... highly recommend it.... Internet has room for improvement, and park owners should install internet boosters...

We stayed here with our motorhome in August 2024.  Such a delightful campground.  Admittedly it was off season and not many caravans around. We were allocated a site backing onto the creek, it was charming watching the wildlife from this location.
The amenities block is well looked after and was clean and spotless. Great facilities with jumping pillow for kids, and a small convenience store nearby.
Wonderful location and amenities. Very clean, staff very helpful at moving us to a different site more to our liking.
Amazing ✨ clean and beautiful
Not a bad little getaway. No too many places directly close by for food but go down the road to a few good places there. The put right next door is superb must try. Recommend lunch or dinner there.

We were there as a group for the Great Ocean Road Marathon, we did the Glamping option.  It is a beautiful park, great facilities, super super clean, the bathrooms were spotless.  The staff were so accommodating.  Great place, thank you for quickly helping out whenever needed.  I accidently left my airpods, they were posted back to me fast.
Amazing, highly recommend! Will be back next year for the Great Ocean Road festival again!!

We absolutely love Big 4 Wye River. Been visiting a bit in the last 2 years and it never disappoints. We never feel the need to leave the park as there is always something to see or do. Amenities are top notch.
Prices slightly higher on peak weekends, but well worth it

I would give this park 6/5 stars if I could. We stayed in the new 3 bedroom, 2 bathroom units with another family and it was perfect. The kids were entertained from dawn till dusk on the go karts, jumping pillow, playground, basketball court & down by the river.  There was wildlife galore, beautiful beach walks and the view from the pub is amazing. I could not recommend it more.
Amazing place to camp. Beautiful amenities. Such an amazing experience

We camped on a powered site right near the river and it was lovely. They've got such a good set up. The bathrooms are lovely and clean. The camp kitchens (there's multiple) are really well equipped. They're expanding at the minute with more cabins. Big shout out to Michael the maintenance guy... Really nice and helpful.
We had the beach house for the Anzac day long weekend. Clean rooms, good kitchen. Good facilities.

Great location right on the beach. A bit isolated but that is what it is all about.
Good community and wonderful beach.
Thumbs up if helpful.

We stayed for 3 nights on a grassed powered site, only downside to whole trip was we were on dirt with grass sites not being used would have made it better with the rain. The new amenities are fantastic best ever I have seen at a park all big 4 should model their bathrooms on wye river. Kids loved the jumping pillow and pedal carts.  We used this as a base camp to explore great ocean road.||Would recommended this to anyone thinking of staying here.

Great spot.
Shady spot, river site.
Great amenities.

We had an amazing weekend camping getaway at Big 4 Wye River Holiday Park! Andy and the crew there were incredibly friendly, professional and helpful, the park's amenities were top-notch, the proximity to the beach was unbeatable, and the affordability made it even better. From check-in Friday until check-out Sunday, we enjoyed every moment of our stay. Highly recommend for anyone looking for a relaxing and budget-friendly camping experience!
Great choice for families and couples.
The rudest receptionist. Will not be coming here ever.
Been coming here eight years now; it's just starting to become too busy. Still worthwhile but maybe pick your times to go
Absolutely best place to spend the Summer holidays. Everything you need within walking distance, with all sorts of activities for kids & adults all week.
Stopped off for a coffee and a snack at the General Store.  Wish we'd had more time to sty and enjoy this fabulous place.  Maybe get back one day........

Nice location beach nearby only but couldn’t capture its picture. Big common kitchen and nice playground. Kids enjoyed go karting and loved the nature experience with ducks, goats, birds and even kangaroos paid us a visit on the last day. Overall we loved this big4
自己開露營車過來玩，有籃球場，滑板車還有很多袋鼠在最後面後山可以看到超讚的。
We got the brand new 3 bedroom cabin and it was nice and very clean. The rangers are very good too
Safe and quiet place for camping with all the amenities included.

Great for a quick close weekend getaway with kids. Huge jumping pillow and go karts for the kids to go around the place. Close to the beach ⛱️. There's only 2 nearby food places - the pub and the general store. Groceries aren't cheap at the store so we recommend you byo food for cooking!
This is the place of dreams we stay every year

We had the best time at Big 4 Wye River. Such a beautiful spot with everything catered for. Definitely a kids paradise and a peaceful parents retreat. Jumping pillow was the highlight for our 5 your old boy. We’ll be back for sure!!
Nice spot and good facilities. The renovation is going great.
It was a great for family and kids.
Great location thats second to none in Victoria. Kids had a ball. would recommend
Love this park. Fabulous amenities.

We loved our stay at the Wye River Big 4 Holiday Park. The reception staff were very friendly and helpful and very accomodating of some changes we needed to make to our booking. We had booked a number of Cabins, a Safari Cabin and the House on the Hill and they were all fantastic, especially the HOtH (incredible views!) and the Safari Cabin (really makes the stay feel like an adventure), I highly recommend them both. The holiday park itself is very beautiful (as is Wye River itself) and there are lots of onsite activities to entertain the kids. We stayed for two nights and had an amazing time. Highly recommended.

amazing place for location as river meets the sea a convenience store and firewood however i wud say be carefull wen choosing ur campsite as there are alot of muddy areas so ensure u bring a tarp its a shame alot of the sites have lost there grass so thats 1 improvement second improvement is if ur gona hire a firepit its a shame u have to wait hours for it to be delivered . i think bring ur own firepit save money unless they can improve and deliver it strait away. apart from that there was a man working there he was fantastic! he told me to move to a grassy area and ditch the muddy site which is what you call 10:10 customer service the girl at the reception had no idea what she was doing but she did try to help atleast i wud definitly stay here again!

Check in was easy and we were given lots of useful information about the Great Ocean Road. The grounds and facilities were exceptional and well maintained. We booked for 2 nights but stayed for 3. Would recommend this park to family and friends.

This place is exvellent. beautifull location, freindly staff, all amenities would seem brand new. local wildlife - parrots and roos wander the grounds and interact with the campers. loved it. used the park as a based to explore the GOR from Torquay to Apollo Bay. 100m walk for a pub meal overlooking the ocean. After 3 nights I was sorry to have to leave.
Sehr guter Campingplatz, mit sauberen Toiletten und Duschen.
This place is my childhood. So many memories. Pretty expensive but lots for kids, beautiful river and great for hanging with mates.

A fantastic almost self-contained park.

Check In: Smooth and friendly providing all the info we needed and gave us the best available position to suit our van size etc.

Site: Nice wide roads allowing for easy reversing with the river on our doorstep. Plenty of ducks and kangaroos visiting us.

Amenities: New toilets blocks new and very clean with a seperate family one to safely take your little guys for washes and toilets. All new washers and driers with tap and go payment. The camp kitchens are awesome to say the least. an indoor and semi outdoor all new.

There is an activities centre and games room with anything from car racing to air hockey. Peddle karts, basketball and beach and water sport hire all available. The river itself is very shallow and would be safe with parents watching for the littlest to play in.

Location: The pub is 50m up the road with a fair range of local beers and awesome views. We didn't try the food as I couldn't find a positive review. There is a sensational grocery store opposite with everything and more available and a cafe serving great food all opposite the beach. Other than that is a 20min drive either way to other shops.

Other Tips: Bring or book a fire pit in the colder months and the local store has plenty of firewood. Don't bother with bikes unless your super steep hill fit. But bring your beach and surf gear and hopefully you will get some waves. Other than that it a fairly self-contact town.

Go Stay There!

The Wye river big 4 has the most amazing customer service continuously. Not only a majestic spot but the out of this world customer service make it a holiday of all holidays.
We travel as a family quite a bit and stay at many big 4s but this one stands out by far! I’m so completely impressed and would highly recommend
